title: Frontend Product Engineer, AI Applications
company_focus:
Applied AI technology company deploying AI-native operations
for large enterprises
description:
About Distyl AI

Distyl is an applied AI technology company partnering with
the world’s most ambitious institutions to rearchitect
critical operations for the frontier of AI. Our customers
include the largest companies in telecom, healthcare,
insurance, manufacturing, consumer goods, and global social
organizations. We research and deploy technologies that
power AI-native operations — both for our partners and for
Distyl itself. Our work spans research into self-
constructing systems, the development of the most reliable
execution of AI systems, and products that transform
mission-critical workflows. As a result, Distyl's
technologies affect some of the world's largest operations —
from hundreds of millions of consumer interactions to tens
of millions of supply chain transactions and millions of
patient journeys. Distyl is backed by leading investors
including Lightspeed Venture Partners, Khosla Ventures,
Coatue, DST Global, and the board-members of 20+ F500s. The
results reflect this approach: a 100% production deployment
success rate for our customers and one of the few enterprise
AI companies to run a profitable business.

What We Are Looking For

We are looking for Frontend Product Engineers to build these
ai applications. This role sits at the intersection of
engineering, product, and AI systems. You will work closely
with customer domain experts, AI strategists, AI
Researchers, and the Platform Engineering Team to design and
ship interfaces that allow humans to guide, evaluate, and
improve AI systems in production environments. Many of these
applications begin as forward-deployed tools for specific
customers. The best ideas and patterns are then incorporated
back into the Distyl platform.

If you enjoy building products quickly, working directly
with users, and experimenting with new AI-native ways of
developing software, this role is for you.

Key Responsibilities

- Design and ship interfaces where operators interact with AI
  systems to perform real operational work. These
  applications may include: knowledge capture tools,
  evaluation and feedback workflows, AI configuration and
  control interfaces, workflow orchestration tools, and
  custom applications for enterprise use cases
- Collaborate closely with AI Strategists and customer domain
  experts to understand real workflows
- Translate those workflows into working product experiences
  that allow operators to interact with and guide AI systems
- Move quickly from idea → prototype → production
- Use real user interactions and system behavior to refine
  both the product experience and the AI systems it supports
- Integrate applications with LLMs, evaluation pipelines, and
  internal AI infrastructure
- Understand how interface design affects: system behavior,
  feedback quality, user trust, system improvement loops

Who You Are

- Early-career builders (0–3 years of software engineering
  experience)
- Comfortable building modern web applications using tools
  such as TypeScript, React, modern component libraries,
  API-driven architectures
- Sharp eye for detail and a natural sense for what makes a
  product sing
- Care about how a workflow feels effortless, how an interface
  guides people naturally, how the right design choice can
  elevate an entire experience
- No design background required — just a real passion for
  products done well and the confidence to push for that
  standard
- Actively use AI tools in your daily workflow for writing and
  debugging code, generating prototypes, exploring designs,
  and accelerating iteration
- Excited about experimenting with new ways of building
  software with AI
- Comfortable starting with an unclear problem and working
  with users to figure out what should be built
- Enjoy working with real users and understanding operational
  problems

What We Offer

- The base salary range for this role is $150K – $250K,
  depending on experience, location, and level. In addition
  to base compensation, this role is eligible for meaningful
  equity, along with a comprehensive benefits package
- 100% covered medical, dental, and vision for employees and
  dependents
- 401(k) with additional perks (e.g., commuter benefits,
  in‑office lunch)
- Access to state‑of‑the‑art models, generous usage of modern
  AI tools, and real‑world business problems
- Ownership of high‑impact projects across top enterprises
- A mission‑driven, fast‑moving culture that prizes curiosity,
  pragmatism, and excellence

Distyl has offices in San Francisco and New York. This role
follows a hybrid collaboration model with 3+ days per week
(Tuesday–Thursday) in‑office.

Move quickly from idea to prototype to production deployment

interact with, guide, evaluate, and improve AI systems in
production. It includes integrating applications with LLMs,
evaluation pipelines, and AI infrastructure, making it
hands-on work deploying and working with AI systems.
